scaled_oracle_cases.description: Combined test covering missing optional columns, malformed data, dedup constraint, and whitespace in column names.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The function should handle missing optional columns, malformed annotations, dedup on repeated row processing, and whitespace in column names.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: When header_dict contains whitespace in keys, missing optional columns (aggr_prec_Fragment_Annotation and Charge), and a row has empty fragment annotation (single semicolon) that leads to early return, and the same normal row is processed twice, the mappings should reflect correct defaults (charge '0', empty pr_transitions), no duplicates in sequences_mapping, and column names should be stripped for lookup.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: If function does not strip whitespace, it will miss columns; if it does not check for missing columns, KeyError; if it does not dedup, duplicates appear; if it does not handle empty annotation early return, it may incorrectly process.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
mapRow(row1, header_dict, precursors_mapping, sequences_mapping, protein_mapping)
mapRow(row2, header_dict, precursors_mapping, sequences_mapping, protein_mapping)
mapRow(row2, header_dict, precursors_mapping, sequences_mapping, protein_mapping)
print('Precursors Mapping:', precursors_mapping)
print('Sequences Mapping:', sequences_mapping)
print('Protein Mapping:', protein_mapping)
covered_requirements: Add a test case where optional columns are missing and verify no error occurs.
Add test cases with malformed data and verify correct handling.
Add a test case that calls mapRow twice with identical row and verifies uniqueness.
Add test case with whitespace in column names and verify correct parsing.
